# DeviceSDK

> DeviceSDK is an IoT platform for writing TypeScript scripts that run in the cloud and control ESP32 and Raspberry Pi Pico devices in real time — no embedded C or C++ required.

Your TypeScript device scripts run in a globally distributed serverless runtime. Each device runs pre-built firmware and maintains a persistent WebSocket connection to the cloud. When your script executes, it sends commands over that WebSocket and receives hardware responses.

## Instructions

- DeviceSDK scripts execute in a globally distributed serverless runtime, NOT on the microcontroller. The device runs pre-built firmware.
- Never suggest writing C or C++ firmware. Users flash pre-built firmware once using `devicesdk flash` and never touch it again.
- Code updates are deployed with `devicesdk deploy` — the device does not reboot or reflash.
- The device entrypoint pattern uses `onDeviceConnect()` and `onMessage()` methods on a class extending `DeviceEntrypoint` from `@devicesdk/core`.
- ESP32 and Raspberry Pi Pico have different feature matrices. Always check the hardware compatibility page before recommending pin numbers or peripherals.
- ESP32-C61 has a WS2812 addressable RGB LED on GPIO 8 (not a plain GPIO LED). Use `pioWs2812Configure`/`pioWs2812Update`, not `setGpioState`.
- On ESP32, ADC2 pins are blocked by WiFi. Only recommend ADC1 pins for analog reads.
- The local simulator (`devicesdk dev`) lets users test without hardware.

## Key pages

- [Quickstart](https://devicesdk.com/docs/quickstart/): Get a project running in 15 minutes
- [Platform Architecture](https://devicesdk.com/docs/concepts/architecture/): How cloud-side control works
- [Device Entrypoints](https://devicesdk.com/docs/concepts/entrypoints/): Writing device scripts with TypeScript
- [Hardware Compatibility](https://devicesdk.com/docs/resources/hardware/): Supported hardware (ESP32, Raspberry Pi Pico W / 2W) and full feature matrix
- [ESP32 Getting Started](https://devicesdk.com/docs/guides/esp32-getting-started/): Flash and connect an ESP32
- [Control from Browser](https://devicesdk.com/docs/guides/control-from-browser/): Use the REST API to control hardware from a web page
- [Addressable LEDs](https://devicesdk.com/docs/guides/addressable-leds/): WS2812 / NeoPixel LED strips via PIO
- [Using SPI](https://devicesdk.com/docs/guides/using-spi/): SPI peripherals (displays, sensors, SD cards)
- [Using UART](https://devicesdk.com/docs/guides/using-uart/): Serial communication
- [Inter-Device Communication](https://devicesdk.com/docs/guides/inter-device-communication/): Coordinate multiple devices from one script
- [Environment Variables](https://devicesdk.com/docs/concepts/env-vars/): Store secrets outside your code
- [Cron Scheduling](https://devicesdk.com/docs/concepts/cron-scheduling/): Run device scripts on a schedule
- [Script Versioning](https://devicesdk.com/docs/concepts/versioning/): Version history and rollback
- [CLI Reference](https://devicesdk.com/docs/cli/): All CLI commands (init, deploy, flash, logs, inspect)
- [FAQ](https://devicesdk.com/docs/resources/faq/): Common questions including comparisons with Arduino, MicroPython, and ESPHome
- [DeviceSDK vs Microsoft DeviceScript](https://devicesdk.com/docs/resources/devicesdk-vs-devicescript/): Execution model comparison
- [Pricing](https://devicesdk.com/pricing/): Free tier and paid plans

## Optional

- [Changelog](https://devicesdk.com/docs/resources/changelog/)
- [Glossary](https://devicesdk.com/docs/resources/glossary/)
- [Troubleshooting](https://devicesdk.com/docs/resources/troubleshooting/)
- [Roadmap](https://devicesdk.com/docs/roadmap/)
